claim: 30Y Treasury yield closes at or above 5.25% at least once before June 17, 2026.
falsification_criteria: The 30Y yield closes below 5.25% on every trading session through June 17.
grading_note: Fiscal premium re-pricing has momentum and no visible catalyst to arrest it; 10bps of additional upside from 5.147% is a conservative extrapolation of a move already in progress. The market has not priced a credible US fiscal consolidation, so the path of least resistance for the long end remains higher.

claim: SLV's total return from May 18 outperforms GLD's by at least 5 percentage points by June 15, 2026.
falsification_criteria: SLV's return from May 18 minus GLD's return from May 18 is below +5pp on June 15.
grading_note: Silver's -10.3% weekly collapse is a velocity outlier driven by the dollar surge; the implied gold/silver ratio is at a historically stretched level that has consistently mean-reverted within weeks once dollar momentum plateaus. Industrial demand underpinning silver is intact and not the source of the dislocation.

claim: XLK beats XLE on total return (both from May 18) by at least 2pp by August 16, 2026.
falsification_criteria: XLE's total return from May 18 meets or exceeds XLK's total return minus 2pp on August 16.
grading_note: Energy's +23% 20-day supply-shock surge has already priced a significant disruption premium; absent oil continuing to rise from here, XLE faces mean-reversion headwinds. The AI capex cycle underpinning tech is structural, and quarterly pullbacks after large runs have consistently been absorbed within the same quarter.

claim: The 3M-10Y Treasury spread (now +105bps) widens to at least 135bps by August 16, 2026.
falsification_criteria: The 3M-10Y spread fails to close at or above 135bps on any single day through August 16.
grading_note: Fiscal premium continues lifting the 10Y while the 3M is anchored by a Fed on hold; 30bps of additional steepening is a conservative ask given the structural drivers of the current re-steepening are unchanged. The market is not pricing a credible mechanism to halt the long-end drift.

claim: EEM underperforms SPY by at least 5 percentage points on total return (both from May 18) by August 16, 2026.
falsification_criteria: EEM's total return from May 18 is within 5pp of SPY's total return on August 16.
grading_note: Oil-importing EM economies face a twin squeeze: DXY near 99 tightens external financing conditions while oil +23% in 20 days expands import bills simultaneously. Historical analogs of this combination — dollar surge plus energy supply shock — have reliably produced EM equity underperformance within one to two quarters.

claim: The Fed implements no rate cut through its November 2026 FOMC meeting, leaving the target range unchanged from May 2026.
falsification_criteria: The Fed announces a cut of at least 25bps at any FOMC meeting on or before November 2026.
grading_note: The oil shock keeps near-term inflation risk alive precisely when the curve has normalized, removing the inversion-as-recession-signal urgency for emergency action; the 30Y at 5.15% signals fiscal concern that a dovish pivot would only worsen. Consensus is pricing easing probability that current data does not yet support.

claim: DXY closes below 95.00 at least once before May 18, 2027.
falsification_criteria: DXY closes at or above 95.00 on every trading day through May 18, 2027.
grading_note: The week's DXY rally to 99 is a tactical bounce, not a structural reversal; US twin deficits, fiscal premium concerns that crowd out foreign capital, and eventual Fed easing create medium-term dollar headwinds that should reassert within twelve months. A 4% decline from current levels is a modest ask if the structural bears are right.

claim: The 30Y Treasury yield's calendar-year 2027 daily-close average exceeds 5.00%.
falsification_criteria: The arithmetic mean of all 30Y Treasury daily closes during calendar year 2027 is 5.00% or below.
grading_note: US deficit trajectory near 6-7% of GDP and a rising debt/GDP path have structurally re-established term premium in a way that requires either credible fiscal consolidation — politically improbable — or a recession severe enough to force emergency easing to reverse. Starting at 5.147% today, the 30Y would need a sustained rally of more than 15bps held across the entire year to disprove this.
